essence: David asks how to reliably resume the same Claude Code session, track which session is active, and close out cleanly — surfacing a missing session-management procedure for multi-machine work.

Debugging Claude Code over SSH to the M2: brew install tree succeeded remotely, but asking it to connect via tmux failed with false claims that tmux and Homebrew were missing — proving the remote SSH shell environment isn't loading correctly.

actions.text: Fix SSH shell environment loading so remote Claude Code sees Homebrew/tmux

essence: David narrates a recorded demo of controlling multiple Macs (M4 Mini, M2) with Claude Code over SSH and screen sharing, showing that auth dialogs triggered remotely pop up on the remote machine's local screen.
David voices intent to set up KyberBot (mis-transcribed as 'Ibobot by Carbonese' = KyberBot by Kybernesis), citing its excellent second-brain memory capabilities.

actions.text: Set up KyberBot for second-brain memory

essence: Bug report on the workflow web UI: selecting the new-incident-analysis workflow still shows and runs the YouTube launch optimizer, all 15 steps display instead of section-filtered steps (top and left sidebar), and the start page should show each workflow's absolute data-folder path with a copy button.

actions.text: File issue: incident workflow selection runs YouTube launch optimizer instead
Filter step navigation to only the current section's steps (top bar and left sidebar)
Show absolute data-folder paths with copy buttons per workflow on the start page

essence: David directs that Angela's docs and possibly the software package be updated so she has a simple, streamlined way to run the incident-workflow application, since her current process is cumbersome.

actions.text: Update Angela's docs and package for an easy one-step way to run the app

essence: Remote session with Angela getting the incident-workflow web UI running with real Claude token auth instead of stub mode (export token, npm run wui), verifying prompts and predicates now execute with clean context isolation for true prompt-quality testing; meeting moved from 12:00 to 13:00 Bangkok time.

actions.text: Stabilise the more advanced (currently broken) web UI version before handing to Angela

essence: David requests a comprehensive factsheet of all troubleshooting work done so far, including full absolute file paths, to hand back to SupportSignal as evidence of the work completed on his side.

actions.text: Compile troubleshooting factsheet with absolute file paths for SupportSignal handoff

essence: David demos POEM at a Chiang Mai meetup: deterministic context-isolated workflows (chain + parallel prompting, YAML/Handlebars/JSON compiled to a single runnable JavaScript blob, n8n-compatible React Flow UI) with agents Penny/Alex/Oscar, plus his second-brain + Lisa-librarian + voice-agent stack, promising future YouTube videos on it.

actions.text: Send Adam the OMI recording once processing finishes

loop.label: publish POEM and librarian YouTube videos

essence: Chiang Mai open-source self-hosting meetup covering Coolify, local LLM serving (Ollama/MLX), nanoGPT cheap routing, Cap as a self-hosted Loom alternative, and Kokoro/Piper/Whisper voice stacks; David shares his Lisa file-based-RAG librarian pattern and wants an open-source Ecamm-style scene switcher controllable from Claude Code with Stream Deck.

David asks why the M4 and another Mac still don't match despite SSH, settings, and plugins being installed via Ansible, and whether Claude over SSH should diagnose the config drift between both systems.

actions.text: Compare both machines' configs via Claude over SSH to diagnose drift
